page: Microsoft Azure NG32ads_V620_v1 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine from the NGADSV620v1 family, powered by an AMD processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, ensuring dedicated compute resource allocation.

For accelerated workloads, the server is equipped with a single AMD V620 GPU providing 30 GB of dedicated VRAM. Storage capabilities are defined by a substantial local disk configuration of 4889 GB, and the instance is premium storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk operations. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this configuration.

Given the combination of 32 dedicated vCPUs, a dedicated V620 GPU, and nearly 5 TB of local storage, this server is designed for resource-intensive workloads. The hardware profile is highly suited for GPU-accelerated databases, remote visualization, machine learning inference, and data-intensive processing tasks that benefit from local storage speeds and dedicated graphics processing.
description: Microsoft Azure NG32ads_V620_v1 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine belonging to the NGADSV620v1 family, utilizing an AMD x86_64 processor. The instance provides 32 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core), and is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Acceleration is delivered by a single AMD V620 GPU with 30 GB of VRAM. For storage-heavy operations, the server features 4889 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage. This hardware configuration is optimized for workloads requiring a balance of dedicated compute, GPU acceleration, and high-capacity local storage, such as database hosting, machine learning inference, and graphics rendering.
og_description: Microsoft Azure NG32ads_V620_v1 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, an AMD V620 GPU with 30 GB of VRAM, and 4889 GB of local storage.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual machine combining dedicated compute cores, high-capacity local storage, and professional graphics hardware for demanding workloads.
